summary: Unmerged tooling PR that introduced `tools/convert_data.py`, a script for converting runs of `.4byte` data in assembly into more semantic `.ascii`/`.asciz`, `.float`, `.int`, `NULL`, or canonical hex forms. The PR also carried a demonstration/application against `sysdolphin/baselib/baselib_unknown_002`, adding many provisional prototypes, Dolphin OS reset/PAD headers, reset-switch structs, and decomp.me scratch links while removing unnecessary `.global` directives from local labels. The PR was closed because it was superseded by #454.
what_changed: Added `tools/convert_data.py`, defaulting to operate on `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/baselib_unknown_002.s` and scanning consecutive `.4byte` or `NULL` lines into a byte buffer before rewriting them in more readable assembly directives.
The conversion script detects zero dwords, printable NUL-terminated ASCII strings, plausible big-endian `f32`, small positive `u32`, small negative `s32`, and falls back to `.4byte 0xXXXXXXXX`.

The script exposes tunable options for zero handling (`hex`, `int`, `null`, `float`), enabling/disabling ASCII/f32/s32/u32 recognition, minimum ASCII length, integer ranges, float magnitude range, and maximum float digit count.

Applied or staged decomp scaffolding in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/baselib_unknown_002.c`: added includes for OS reset, OS reset switch, MSL, and OSMemory; added provisional structs/callback typedefs; refined some callback pointer signatures; and declared many external labels/functions with more specific types.
Added `include/dolphin/os/OSReset.h` declaring `OSResetSystem` and moved the declaration out of `include/dolphin/os/init/__start.h` into that header.
Added `include/dolphin/os/OSResetSW.h` with an `OSResetSW_Unk1` layout, reset-switch predicate typedefs, multicast predicate typedef, and `OSGetResetSwitchState` declaration.
Added minimal source stubs `src/dolphin/os/OSReset.c` and `src/dolphin/os/OSResetSW.c` that include the new headers and record address-commented declarations for the still-undecompiled functions.
Expanded `include/dolphin/pad/pad.h` with `PADClamp`, `PADRead`, and `PADReset` declarations and normalized spacing in `PADStatus` fields.
Removed a commented unknown-function line for `func_80397520` from `include/functions.h` while adding a typed extern in the baselib source.

Removed many `.global` directives from local labels in `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/baselib_unknown_002.s`, including jump-table case labels and simple `blr` labels, while adding decomp.me scratch comments for several remaining functions/labels.
Added a decomp.me scratch comment above `OSGetResetSwitchState` in `asm/dolphin/os/OSResetSW.s`.
smart_moves: The data conversion tool uses a conservative fallback path: if a 4-byte chunk does not satisfy ASCII, zero, float, signed-int, or unsigned-int heuristics, it remains a canonical `.4byte 0xXXXXXXXX` instead of guessing.

ASCII detection requires printable or line-feed bytes, rejects strings starting with zero, enforces a configurable minimum length, escapes backslashes and newlines, and emits either `.asciz` or `.ascii` plus `.balign 4` depending on alignment needs.
Float detection filters out zero, NaN, very tiny values, very large values, and values with too many digits, reducing false positives from arbitrary binary data.
The tool batches consecutive `.4byte` lines into a buffer before decoding, allowing string detection across word boundaries rather than treating each dword independently.
Header additions moved ad hoc declarations toward subsystem headers (`OSReset.h`, `OSResetSW.h`, `pad.h`) instead of keeping everything in central or local declarations.
The asm cleanup distinguishes externally relevant symbols from local jump-table/internal labels by removing `.global` on many local labels, which can reduce symbol pollution.
Decomp.me scratch URLs were recorded directly next to related assembly labels/functions, preserving matching research context for future decompilers.
decomp_lessons: When raw `.4byte` data may actually encode strings, floats, small integers, or `NULL`, a configurable conversion pass can make assembly data sections far easier to read before or during C reconstruction.
For decomp tooling that guesses data types, provide strict thresholds and per-type toggles so users can tune false-positive risk per file or section.
Convert runs of data, not isolated words, when looking for strings; string boundaries commonly cross 4-byte directive boundaries.
Use `.asciz` only when the string plus terminator naturally consumes the word alignment; otherwise pair `.ascii` with `.balign 4` to preserve layout.
Avoid exporting local assembly labels with `.global` unless they are real externally referenced symbols; jump-table case labels and trivial branch-return labels generally should remain local.
Move known library prototypes into domain headers as soon as possible, even if implementations are still assembly-backed, to reduce duplicate extern declarations and improve type propagation.
Provisional struct fields should use offset-based names and padding arrays when the layout is known but semantics are not, as seen in `OSResetSW_Unk1` fields like `x54_buffer`, `xBC`, and `xD4`.
Record decomp.me scratch links in nearby comments when a match attempt exists, but treat them as transient research notes unless the function is actually decompiled and reviewed.
naming_conventions: `convert_data.py` uses option names such as `find_ascii`, `find_f32`, `find_s32`, `find_u32`, `min_ascii_len`, `s32_min_value`, `u32_max_value`, and `f32_max_abs_value`, making conversion heuristics explicit and searchable.
Unknown struct and callback names follow the project’s provisional pattern: `OSResetSW_Unk1`, `baselib_Unk17`, `baselib_Unk18`, `baselib_UnkCallback1`, and `baselib_UnkCallbackContainer1`.
Unknown struct members use offset-prefixed names such as `x0_maybe_color`, `xC_buffer`, `x54_buffer`, `xBC`, `xC4`, `xD0`, and `xD4`.
Padding fields are named by byte ranges, e.g. `x10_pad[0x14 - 0x10]`, `x58_pad[0x5E - 0x58]`, and `xC0_pad[0xC4 - 0xC0]`.
Function pointer typedefs include semantic hints when available, such as `OSResetSW_Predicate` and `OSResetSW_MulticastPredicate`, but weaker guesses are kept provisional.
A source comment notes `baselib_UnkCallback1` is possibly `OSSwitchThreadCallback`, indicating uncertainty rather than prematurely renaming it.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added decomp.me scratch comments next to assembly entry points: `OSGetResetSwitchState`, `func_80393D2C`, `func_80396A20`, `func_80396C78`, `lbl_80396E40`, `lbl_80397110`, `func_80397520`, and `func_803975D4`.
Annotated a group of trivial `blr` labels with `https://decomp.me/scratch/blr`, implying they may correspond to empty callback functions or return-only stubs.

Removed `.global` from many internal labels in `baselib_unknown_002.s`, especially jump-table dispatch labels like `lbl_80392D28` through `lbl_80392DD0` and reset-switch case labels like `lbl_80396CEC` through `lbl_80396D34`.
Used address comments in C extern declarations, such as `/* 00394100 */ extern void func_80397520(baselib_Unk17 *);`, to keep C prototypes tied to known assembly locations.
The conversion script reads and writes the target assembly file in place, which is useful for bulk cleanup but requires version-control review of generated diffs.
review_feedback: There were no review comments in the provided slice.
The only issue comment states: "Superseded by #454."
Because the PR was closed unmerged and superseded, the proposed script and demo edits should be treated as historical context rather than accepted project policy.
